title: Free Things to Do With Kids in Wellington
meta_desc: Discover the best free and cheap things to do with kids in Wellington — Te Papa, the waterfront, beaches and bush walks that won't cost a cent. Family-tested.
intro: Wellington might be New Zealand's compact capital, but it punches well above its weight when it comes to free family fun. We've spent many happy days here exploring Te Papa, walking the waterfront, riding the cable car (well, that one's cheap) and discovering hidden bush playgrounds. Here are our favourite free and cheap things to do with kids in Wellington.

sections.heading: Te Papa & Free Museums
sections.body:
Te Papa, New Zealand's national museum, is completely free and is hands-down one of the best family attractions in the country. Our kids love the colossal squid, the earthquake house that shakes, and the interactive natural history exhibits. You could spend a whole rainy day here and not see it all — and it won't cost a thing (a few special exhibitions are ticketed, but the bulk is free).

The City Gallery Wellington and the Cable Car Museum at the top of the cable car are also free to enter.
list: Te Papa Tongarewa — free national museum, all-day fun
Cable Car Museum — free, at the top of the cable car
City Gallery Wellington — free general admission
Wellington Museum on the waterfront — free entry

sections.heading: Free Waterfront & Beaches
sections.body:
Wellington's waterfront is a free playground in itself. The kids love jumping off the diving platform at Frank Kitts Park in summer, exploring the wooden Whairepo Lagoon walkways, and running through the splash fountains. There's a brilliant free playground at Frank Kitts Park with a lighthouse slide.

For beaches, Oriental Bay is a short walk from the city with golden imported sand, while Scorching Bay and Days Bay (a ferry ride away) are local family favourites.
list: Frank Kitts Park playground — lighthouse slide, free
Waterfront walk — fountains, sculptures, boats
Oriental Bay beach — sand, calm water, close to city
Scorching Bay & Days Bay — local family beaches

sections.heading: Free Bush Walks & Nature
sections.body:
Wellington is wrapped in green belt and the walks are free and family-friendly. The Botanic Garden is gorgeous and free — take the cable car up (the only paid bit, and worth it) then walk down through the gardens, past the duck pond and the playground. Otari-Wilton's Bush has easy boardwalk trails through native forest, and the kids love spotting tūī and kākā.

Zealandia eco-sanctuary has an entry fee, but the surrounding Wright's Hill and Tinakori Hill tracks are free with great views.
list: Wellington Botanic Garden — free, walk down from cable car
Otari-Wilton's Bush — free native forest boardwalks
Mount Victoria lookout — free 360-degree city views
Tinakori Hill tracks — free bush walks, birdlife

sections.heading: Cheap Family Extras
sections.body: A couple of Wellington classics cost very little. The cable car is a Wellington must-do and is cheap for a family, especially with the free museum and gardens at the top. The interislander ferry terminal and the wharf are free to watch the big ships come and go, and the weekend Harbourside Market is free to wander (and great for cheap snacks).
list: Wellington Cable Car — cheap family fare, NZ icon
Harbourside Market (weekends) — free to browse, cheap eats
Watch the Interislander ferries — free at the wharf
Capital E and the library — free play and story sessions

sections.heading: Budget Summary
sections.body: Wellington is one of the easiest NZ cities to enjoy on a tight budget thanks to free Te Papa and the compact, walkable centre. Here's how a family day adds up. With Te Papa and the waterfront alone, you've got a full, memorable day for almost nothing.
list: Te Papa & free museums: FREE
Waterfront & playgrounds: FREE
Bush walks & beaches: FREE
Botanic Garden: FREE
Cable car (optional): ~$10 family return
Pack-your-own picnic: ~$20 for a family of four
Estimated full-day cost: $0–$30
